title: ranunculus obliquifolius
intro: Ranunculus obliquifolius, also known as lesser buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus obliquifolius are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes.
